privacy.title: How Killio handles account, workspace, and product data.
privacy.intro: This page explains what information Killio collects, why it is processed, and the controls available to account holders and workspace admins.
privacy.contact: If your team needs a data request, deletion request, or contract review, use your agreed support channel with Kynto and include your workspace identifier.

collection.title: Information we collect
collection.body: Killio stores profile details, authentication credentials, workspace content, activity history, and basic operational logs needed to run the service securely.

usage.title: Why we use it
usage.body: We use collected data to authenticate users, deliver collaboration features, improve product reliability, and provide support requested by workspace operators.

sharing.title: When data is shared
sharing.body: Killio does not sell personal data. Information is shared only with infrastructure and service providers required to operate the platform or when lawfully required.

retention.title: Retention
retention.body: Account and workspace records are kept while the service relationship remains active and for a limited period afterward when needed for security, billing, or legal obligations.

rights.title: Your controls
rights.body: Users can request account updates through their workspace admin, while admins can request export or deletion workflows according to the terms governing their workspace.

terms.title: Rules for using Killio across teams, boards, documents, and AI-assisted workflows.
terms.intro: These terms define acceptable use, account responsibilities, and service boundaries when accessing Killio provided by Kynto.
terms.contact: Commercial, legal, or security questions about these terms should be routed through the support contact established for your workspace or deployment.

access.title: Service access
access.body: Access to Killio may be limited, suspended, or updated to protect platform stability, customer environments, or compliance obligations.

accounts.title: Account responsibilities
accounts.body: Users are responsible for accurate registration information, credential security, and activity performed under their account or invited workspace membership.

acceptableUse.title: Acceptable use
acceptableUse.body: You may not use Killio to disrupt the service, violate laws, abuse other users, reverse engineer protected systems, or submit harmful or unauthorized content.

ownership.title: Ownership and content
ownership.body: Workspace customers retain ownership of their content. Killio and Kynto retain rights to the platform, software, branding, and service improvements.

termination.title: Termination
termination.body: Accounts or workspaces may be suspended or terminated for non-payment, security risk, misuse, or violation of these terms, subject to applicable contractual obligations.

cookies.title: How browser storage is used for sessions, preferences, and product reliability.
cookies.intro: Killio uses cookies and similar storage mechanisms to keep sessions active, remember preferences, and improve operational reliability.
cookies.contact: If your organization needs additional detail about storage behavior or consent handling, contact Kynto through your standard support process.

whatTheyAre.title: What cookies are
whatTheyAre.body: Cookies are small browser records that help applications recognize a session, remember settings, and reduce repeated friction across visits.

howWeUse.title: How Killio uses them
howWeUse.body: Killio uses browser storage for authentication continuity, locale preferences, account switching state, and certain product experience settings.

choices.title: Your choices
choices.body: You can control cookies through browser settings, but disabling required storage may limit sign-in, workspace access, or other core product functions.

thirdParties.title: Third-party services
thirdParties.body: Some integrated infrastructure providers may set or rely on storage needed for delivery, security, media handling, or analytics required to operate the service.

updates.title: Policy updates
updates.body: This policy may change as product capabilities, infrastructure choices, or legal requirements evolve. Material updates will be reflected in the date above.
